body.u: I am now sixty and my life has gone itsit's not worth living any longer I’ve had enough. When I was sixteen I left school, my {illegible} told me to stay on but I couldn’t be bothered I just wanted to lifelive from day to day. I suppose there wasn’t much enthusiasm behind me but that life. From sixteen tooto twenty it was a good time no job and just sponging money from my dad and he was a builder. My mum wanted me to go in the NavelNavy but again I couldn’t be bothered. From 16-20 and even now at 60 I never married, I never did get on with girls ait never lasterlasted long with them. I got a car, a ford, it was an old banger and my old man said I should keep it in good working order, but I wanted to go grass racing with it so it was taken away. So I asked for a bike, motor bike. It was the first day on it and got that taken away because I went to made on it.

After twenty I had a job as an adertizeradvertiser because that was the only thing I was good at. NothinkNothing really happened for a few years just the same old continuous life, having no real enformentenforcement and wonder what it was all about.

Really I didn’t nowknow what life was all about or religion. My mum was a Jehavah’sJehova's witness and my dad couldn’t be bother, still when I was 30 {illegible} I suddenly relizedrealised I was lobleylonely and I’ve been lonlylonely ever since. I was not married. I didn’t want to get married in the first place because I wanted to prove to the girls I went with that I could live without them just because ever body else needed them i didn’t. I always wanted to be different from every body else in side of going with the majority. I was 40 now and time was moving on I had a fairly nice house and looked after myself fairly well. When I was 50 I realized I would soon be sixty and of all things I never had a penchenpension. My old man said to me that I couldn’t have a penchenpension because I mitemight or he said I probably wouldn’t live that long. Thank you dad. My dad said that they take the money from and they only give you a little at the end and you wouldn’t live much over 60. So that was it.
